title: lotononis jacottetii
intro: Lotononis jacottetii is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa, specifically in the savannas and grasslands. It is a small tree or shrub, commonly known as the 'hairy lotononis' or 'stick tree', which is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically harvested and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal benefits.
